title: Understand: Overview of the Federal Judiciary
description: Check your understanding of the federal judiciary with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Understand: Overview of the Federal Judiciary

assessment_items.question_content:
**The power of the Supreme Court to review the constitutionality of acts of the national and state governments is known as \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**How many justices currently serve on the Supreme Court?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Who served as chief justice of the United States during the landmark decision of *Marbury v. Madison,* which addressed the issue of judicial review?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which TWO statements describe the Supreme Court’s ruling in *Marbury v. Madison*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Court said that state governments have no authority to pass laws that interfere with the federal government's powers.
The Court admitted that it had no authority to order Madison to deliver Marbury’s commission.
The Court affirmed the power of judicial review and used it to declare that a part of the Judiciary Act was unconstitutional.
The Court ruled in favor of Marbury, commanding Secretary of State James Madison to deliver his commission.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Justices who would rule differently than the Court’s majority can still issue their own opinions on the case. These opinions are known as  \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these would likely be heard by the Supreme Court?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A case which a driver is contesting a speeding ticket
A case in which a person is accused of robbing a bank
A case that involves conflicting interpretations of the First Amendment
A debate over how to regulate fireworks during a Fourth of July celebration

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many justices must agree to hear a case before it can be argued before the Supreme Court?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: None, because justices do not get to decide what cases the Court hears

assessment_items.question_content:
**What do the justices wear while the Supreme Court is in session?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Who is the current chief justice of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

During the Civil War, the Supreme Court consisted of 10 justices. 

**What is one argument against having an even number of justices on the court?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Constitution says there must be nine justices.
There aren’t that many chairs in the courtroom.
The court could be deadlocked on close decisions.
The Supreme Court would be too powerful.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who was the first African American justice to serve on the Supreme Court?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**In what situation would a justice most likely issue a concurring opinion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When they agree with the majority but have additional thoughts about an issue in the case
When they disagree with the majority’s ruling and want to explain why they would rule differently
When they want to send the case down to a lower court, rather than coming to a decision on their own
When they want to suggest a new amendment to the Constitution
